name: App Header
description: A persistent top-level header pattern containing branding, primary navigation, and utility actions across desktop and mobile layouts.

tree: Mobile: header > StackLayout row (menu button + brand) + Drawer (vertical nav + utilities)
BorderItem center (main content offset below fixed header)
orderRules: Branding appears first and links to app home.
Desktop layout positions brand, navigation, and utilities with space-between.
Mobile layout places menu button left of brand and moves nav/utilities into Drawer.
In mobile Drawer, navigation items appear above utility actions.
Header remains sticky/fixed while content scrolls.

variantMatrix.name: Header with vertical navigation
variantMatrix.evidence: Docs: App header -> Header with vertical navigation

layoutRules: Place app header in the north region and keep it sticky/fixed across page scroll.
Default branding to JPM logo in the app header unless the user prompt explicitly specifies another approved brand.
Download logo assets from the logo documentation source and copy them into public/assets/logos before wiring app-header branding.
If a selected logo asset is unavailable at runtime, use simple Amplitude fallback text matching brand (JPM, Chase, or JPMC).
In mobile view, the drawer must render the menu using the VerticalNavigation component with appearance defaulting to bordered.
Mobile drawer padding must be var(--salt-spacing-300) applied equally on all sides (top, right, bottom, left).
When vertical navigation is paired with app header, default its appearance to bordered unless the user prompt explicitly requests another approved variant.
When vertical navigation is used, ensure the container wrapping it has no left padding (padding-left: 0).
Always position logo in the top-left and preserve logo aspect ratio.
Use desktop horizontal navigation for larger viewports and collapse to hamburger + drawer on mobile.
Keep navigation entries above utility actions in mobile drawer hierarchy.
Offset main content by header height so fixed header does not overlap content.
Use skip link before complex header content to bypass navigation and utilities.
dataRequirements: brand: logo asset and optional application name text; brand links to home route and defaults to JPM unless user prompt specifies otherwise.
logoAssetFolder: local folder for installed branding assets (public/assets/logos) and runtime URL mapping under /assets/logos/.
logoFallbackText: simple Amplitude fallback text for missing assets (JPM|Chase|JPMC).
mobileMenuComponent: must use VerticalNavigation component with appearance defaulting to bordered for mobile drawer navigation.
verticalNavigationAppearance: bordered by default for app-header layouts that include vertical navigation.
verticalNavigationContainerPadding: must be 0 whenever vertical navigation component is rendered.
utilityActions: ordered list of global actions (icon, label, handler/route).
activeNavigationKey: currently selected top-level navigation item.
mobileMenuState: open/closed state for drawer presentation.
responsiveBreakpointPolicy: rules for switching desktop vs mobile composition.

gaps.evidence: Some story styles use literal values like width: 100% and z-index arithmetic tied to drawer.
gaps.recommendation: Prefer explicit layout tokens or documented semantic roles for width/stacking in implementation guides.
gaps.label: SOURCE_GAP

gaps.evidence: Docs define header-only and header-with-vertical-navigation layouts, while stories primarily demonstrate one shell with responsive branch.
gaps.recommendation: Represent vertical-navigation pairing as a documented variant inferred from docs plus related navigation pattern evidence.
gaps.label: SOURCE_GAP
